root_text.an3.25:1.3: Arukūpamacitto puggalo, vijjūpamacitto puggalo, vajirūpamacitto puggalo.
root_text.an3.25:1.5: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco puggalo kodhano hoti upāyāsabahulo
root_text.an3.25:1.6: appampi vutto samāno abhisajjati kuppati byāpajjati patitthīyati kopañca dosañca appaccayañca pātukaroti.
root_text.an3.25:1.7: Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, duṭṭhāruko kaṭṭhena vā kaṭhalāya vā ghaṭṭito bhiyyoso mattāya āsavaṁ deti;
root_text.an3.25:1.8: evamevaṁ kho, bhikkhave, idhekacco puggalo kodhano hoti upāyāsabahulo
root_text.an3.25:1.9: appampi vutto samāno abhisajjati kuppati byāpajjati patitthīyati kopañca dosañca appaccayañca pātukaroti.
root_text.an3.25:2.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco puggalo ‘idaṁ dukkhan’ti yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti, ‘ayaṁ dukkhasamudayo’ti yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti, ‘ayaṁ dukkhanirodho’ti yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti, ‘ayaṁ dukkhanirodhagāminī paṭipadā’ti yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti.
root_text.an3.25:2.3: Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, cakkhumā puriso rattandhakāratimisāyaṁ vijjantarikāya rūpāni passeyya;
root_text.an3.25:2.4: evamevaṁ kho, bhikkhave, idhekacco puggalo ‘idaṁ dukkhan’ti yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti …pe… ‘ayaṁ dukkhanirodhagāminī paṭipadā’ti yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti.
root_text.an3.25:3.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco puggalo āsavānaṁ khayā anāsavaṁ cetovimuttiṁ paññāvimuttiṁ diṭṭheva dhamme sayaṁ abhiññā sacchikatvā upasampajja viharati.
root_text.an3.25:3.3: Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, vajirassa natthi kiñci abhejjaṁ maṇi vā pāsāṇo vā;
root_text.an3.25:3.4: evamevaṁ kho, bhikkhave, idhekacco puggalo āsavānaṁ khayā …pe… upasampajja viharati.
root_text.an3.25:3.6: Ime kho, bhikkhave, tayo puggalā santo saṁvijjamānā lokasmin”ti.

translation_text.an3.25:1.1: “These three individuals are found in the world.
translation_text.an3.25:1.3: An individual with a mind like an open sore, an individual with a mind like lightning, and an individual with a mind like diamond.
translation_text.an3.25:1.4: And who has a mind like an open sore?
translation_text.an3.25:1.5: It’s an individual who is irritable and bad-tempered.
translation_text.an3.25:1.6: Even when lightly criticized they lose their temper, becoming annoyed, hostile, and hard-hearted, and they display annoyance, hate, and bitterness.
translation_text.an3.25:1.7: They’re like a festering sore, which, when you hit it with a stick or a stone, discharges even more.
translation_text.an3.25:1.8: In the same way, an individual is irritable and bad-tempered.
translation_text.an3.25:1.9: Even when lightly criticized they lose their temper, becoming annoyed, hostile, and hard-hearted, and they display annoyance, hate, and bitterness.
translation_text.an3.25:1.10: This is called an individual with a mind like an open sore.
translation_text.an3.25:2.1: And who has a mind like lightning?
translation_text.an3.25:2.2: It’s an individual who truly understands: ‘This is suffering’ … ‘This is the origin of suffering’ … ‘This is the cessation of suffering’ … ‘This is the practice that leads to the cessation of suffering’.
translation_text.an3.25:2.3: They’re like a person with keen eyes in the dark of the night, who sees by a flash of lightning.
translation_text.an3.25:2.4: In the same way, an individual truly understands: ‘This is suffering’ … ‘This is the origin of suffering’ … ‘This is the cessation of suffering’ … ‘This is the practice that leads to the cessation of suffering’.
translation_text.an3.25:2.5: This is called an individual with a mind like lightning.
translation_text.an3.25:3.1: And who has a mind like diamond?
translation_text.an3.25:3.2: It’s an individual who realizes the undefiled freedom of heart and freedom by wisdom in this very life. And they live having realized it with their own insight due to the ending of defilements.
translation_text.an3.25:3.3: It’s like a diamond, which can’t be cut by anything at all, not even a gem or a stone.
translation_text.an3.25:3.4: In the same way, an individual realizes the undefiled freedom of heart and freedom by wisdom in this very life. …
translation_text.an3.25:3.5: This is called an individual with a mind like diamond.
translation_text.an3.25:3.6: These are the three individuals found in the world.”

variant_text.an3.25:1.7: duṭṭhāruko → duṭṭhārukā (bj) | kaṭhalāya → kaṭhalena (bj); kathalāya (mr); kaṭhalena-kathalena (aṭṭha.) | ghaṭṭito → ghaṭṭitā (bj) | āsavaṁ deti → assandati (bj); assavanoti (si)
